skill.name: theater-detection-audit
skill.description: Audits codebases for placeholder logic, mock data, stub functions, TODO markers, and incomplete production paths.
skill.summary: Find and prioritize code theater before release.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 22 high-risk-looking patterns, but review found they are false positives from prose and Graphviz labels. The skill contains no executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection text, or malicious instructions. Low risk remains because the intended workflow asks an AI assistant to inspect codebase files and complete implementations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts occur in explanatory documentation about audits, design, error handling, testing, reporting, and Claude Code workflow. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, crypto APIs, password hashing, encryption logic, or instructions to use weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are prose or diagram labels, not executable cryptographic code. Manual review found no cryptographic operations or recommended weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts refer to codebase audit language, such as identifying placeholder work and preparing code for production. No evidence found of host enumeration, operating system probing, credential discovery, or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a code quality audit workflow. There are no commands, scripts, or instructions for machine reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: False Positive: C2 Keywords In Graph Nodes
low_findings.description: The C2 keyword alerts are Graphviz node identifiers and workflow labels for completion steps. No evidence found of command-and-control infrastructure, remote callbacks, payload control, or external communication endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a static Graphviz process diagram. The flagged token is a local node name, not a network or malware control pattern.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Scanning Tool Reference
low_findings.description: The network scanning alert appears on a diagram label about dependency mapping and external systems. No evidence found of nmap, masscan, port scanning, socket probing, or instructions to scan networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line describes dependency analysis in a release-readiness audit. It does not contain a scanning command, target, or network operation.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Review Workflow
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to read scoped codebase context and write production implementations. This is expected for a code audit skill, but users should run it only on repositories where code modification is intended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation clearly requires reading code context and implementing production changes. This is legitimate for the skill, but it is still a real filesystem-impacting capability.

content.value_statement: Prototype code often hides mocks, stubs, and TODO paths that fail in production. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured audit and completion plan.
actual_capabilities: Identifies mock data, hardcoded responses, stub functions, and TODO markers.
Separates obvious pattern matches from context-based implementation gaps.
Builds a dependency map for related placeholder implementations.
Prioritizes findings by production risk, user impact, and completion order.
Produces an audit report with findings, risks, and a completion roadmap.
Guides follow-up implementation and testing for replacement production code.
limitations: It depends on clear repository scope from the user.
It cannot infer unclear product requirements without user input.
It may require access to external service documentation to design replacements.
It is not a substitute for human code review before release.

use_cases.title: Prepare A Prototype For Launch
use_cases.description: Find mocks, hardcoded responses, and simplified paths before a release candidate is approved.

use_cases.title: Assess An Inherited Codebase
use_cases.description: Map what is real, what is placeholder, and which incomplete areas block production use.

use_cases.title: Plan Technical Debt Cleanup
use_cases.description: Turn scattered TODOs and stub functions into a prioritized remediation plan.

prompt_templates.title: Find Placeholder Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the src directory for mock data, stub functions, TODO markers, and hardcoded responses. Report each finding with file location, risk, and recommended next step.

prompt_templates.title: Review Release Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Pre-release review for a product team.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the theater-detection-audit workflow on this repository before release. Focus on authentication, payments, data loading, and error handling. Prioritize findings by production impact.

prompt_templates.title: Map Placeholder Dependencies
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for connected incomplete work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the codebase for placeholder implementations and group related findings by dependency. Show which mocks or stubs must be replaced first to unblock the rest.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Completion Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a full theater detection audit, classify risk, estimate implementation effort, and create a phased roadmap for replacing placeholders with production code and tests.

output_examples.input: Audit a frontend application before launch.
output_examples.output: The report lists hardcoded user data in the dashboard, a fake billing status response, and shallow error handling in checkout. The checkout issue is highest priority because it can hide payment failures.

output_examples.input: Find stubs in an inherited API service.
output_examples.output: The audit identifies empty handlers, TODO comments near authorization checks, and a mocked notification client. It recommends completing authorization first because other handlers depend on it.

output_examples.input: Plan cleanup for a prototype repository.
output_examples.output: The roadmap groups findings into data access, authentication, and testing phases. It flags unclear requirements for deferred items and assigns validation steps for each replacement.

best_practices: Provide a clear audit scope and list high-risk areas first.
Review each finding in context before replacing code.
Add tests that fail against the placeholder and pass against the real implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not treat every TODO as equally urgent.
Do not replace mocks without confirming intended behavior.
Do not mark theater resolved until tests validate real production paths.

faq.question: What does code theater mean?
faq.answer: It means code that appears complete but relies on placeholders, mocks, stubs, or simplified behavior.

faq.question: Can this skill change files?
faq.answer: Yes. Its workflow can guide implementation changes when the user asks to replace placeholders.

faq.question: Does it only find TODO comments?
faq.answer: No. It also looks for mock data, hardcoded responses, empty functions, simplified errors, and test-only behavior.

faq.question: Is it useful for mature codebases?
faq.answer: Yes. It can find old temporary paths and incomplete migrations that remained after earlier releases.

faq.question: Can it verify production behavior?
faq.answer: It can recommend tests and review criteria, but final verification depends on the project and environment.

faq.question: What input should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide repository paths, known risky areas, release goals, and any requirements for the audit report.
